subject: Geology
title: Geologic Hazards
description: Learn Geologic Hazards in one minute.

hook.text: 700,000 people live on the slopes of Mount Vesuvius. It erupted once before. Geologists monitor it daily.

buildup.text: Geologic hazards include earthquakes, volcanic eruptions, tsunamis, landslides, and sinkholes.

discovery.text: Geologists map hazard zones, install monitoring networks, and develop early warning systems to save lives.

twist.text: We can't prevent hazards, but we can predict many of them. The challenge is getting people to listen and act.

climax.text: Geologic hazards are where science meets survival. Understanding Earth's power is the first step to safety.

punchline.text: The planet warns us if we know how to listen.

quiz.question: What do geologists do about hazards?
options: Map hazard zones and develop warning systems
